literature_sources.mechanism: MoE router merging should match source routing distributions; a second-order KL approximation yields a Hessian/input-covariance weighted linear system.
literature_sources.title: When Model Merging Breaks Routing: Training-Free Calibration for MoE

literature_sources.mechanism: Weight averaging is accepted only after validation against source or held-out controls.
literature_sources.title: Model soups: averaging weights of multiple fine-tuned models improves accuracy without increasing inference time

literature_sources.mechanism: Dense-model merging needs explicit interference handling; MoE routers need an additional top-k boundary gate.
literature_sources.title: TIES-Merging: Resolving Interference When Merging Models

literature_sources.mechanism: Activation/statistics-aware closed-form merging is useful, but layer-local statistics must be checked against propagation and validation gates.
literature_sources.title: RegMean++: Enhancing Effectiveness and Generalization of Regression Mean for Model Merging

solver_plan.linear_system: (sum_i E[H_i kron xx^T]) vec(W_m^T) = sum_i E[H_i kron xx^T] vec(W_i^T)
solver_plan.next_command: collect router logits and hidden states for source/candidate prompts, then solve the matrix-free HARC linear system before matched vLLM acceptance
solver_plan.objective: min_Wm sum_i E_x KL(softmax(W_i x) || softmax(W_m x))
solver_plan.quadratic_proxy: 0.5 * (W_m x - W_i x)^T H_i (W_m x - W_i x)
solver_plan.same_shape_constraint: only update existing router tensors; output checkpoint keeps the same architecture and tensor shapes
